fundamentals.name: AST SpaceMobile, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: AST SpaceMobile, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, designs and develops the constellation of BlueBird satellites in the United States. The company provides a cellular broadband network in space to be accessible directly by smartphones for commercial use and other applications, as well as for government use. Its SpaceMobile service provides cellular broadband services to end-users who are out of terrestrial cellular coverage. The company was founded in 2017 and is headquartered in Midland, Texas.

judgment.summary: Extremely high revenue growth is overshadowed by massive operating losses, negative profit margins, and a deeply negative forward P/E.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates by a wide margin (-0.66 vs -0.20); next earnings date is 2026-08-10.
positives: Revenue growth 1952.2% YoY — explosive top-line expansion.
Total cash $3.03B and current ratio 18.47 — strong liquidity position.
Gross margin 44.8% — indicates some pricing power on services rendered.
Total debt $2.99B is nearly matched by cash — manageable leverage.
negatives: Operating margin -1014.0% and Profit margin 0.0% — significant operational losses.
Forward P/E -423.85 — priced for extreme future growth that is not yet realized.
Return on equity -37.8% and Return on assets -6.3% — poor profitability.
Free cash flow (TTM) $-1.41B — burning significant cash.

news.paragraph: AST SpaceMobile's joint venture with US carriers is now focusing on execution risks, following recent market approval for the company. While the company's massive revenue growth of over 1900% year-over-year is notable, its forward P/E is deeply negative and its operating margins are extremely poor. The upcoming earnings report on August 10th will be critical for assessing the company's ability to translate its approvals and growth into tangible operational success.
